display-name.lang: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Tyler Tanner leads No. 10 Vanderbilt against No. 17 Arkansas at Bud Walton Arena. The Razorbacks look to utilize Trevon Brazile's interior defense against Devin McGlockton and a surging Commodores squad in a battle for SEC supremacy.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: From Bud Walton Arena in Fayetteville, Ark.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: From the Lloyd Noble Center in Norman, Okla.

programme.title: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: (If necessary).

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: A battle of elite guards highlights the ice in Starkville as the No.21 Arkansas Razorbacks faces the Mississippi State Bulldogs. The Razorbacks look to maintain their top-four SEC standing, while the Bulldogs aim for a signature home win.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 21 Arkansas visits LSU at the Pete Maravich Assembly Center. The Razorbacks won the Jan. 25 meeting 76-73 behind 31 points from freshman Darius Acuff Jr. The Tigers now rely on senior Max Mackinnon to defend home court in this SEC showdown.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 21 Arkansas hosts Auburn at Bud Walton Arena. Razorback Darius Acuff Jr. headlines against Keyshawn Hall. D. Acuff Jr. shoots 49% from the field, while Hall leads the Tigers. Both teams battle for a top spot in the competitive SEC standings.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 21 Arkansas Razorbacks travel to Tuscaloosa to face the Alabama Crimson Tide in a marquee SEC clash. Alabama's sophomore sensation Labaron Philon Jr. and Arkansas's Darius Acuff Jr. are both among the league's top scorers.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Missouri travels to Fayetteville to take on No. 20 Arkansas. Arkansas sits near the top of the SEC standings, while Missouri competes for positioning in the middle of the rankings. Darius Acuff Jr. leads Arkansas in scoring and assists.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 20 Arkansas (20-7) hosts Texas A&M (19-8) at Bud Walton Arena. The Aggies seek a third straight win after beating Oklahoma 75-71. John Calipari and the Razorbacks look to high scorer Darius Acuff Jr. to lead the home offense in the SEC matchup.

programme.title: Fishing: On the Bank
programme.desc: Angling magazine show presented by Rob Hughes and Andy Ford, featuring coverage of the 2025 Blue Fin Tuna Championship.

programme.title: TNT Sports Reload
programme.desc: The best bits from TNT Sports over the last week, including exclusive interviews, top action, and more.

programme.title: International T20 Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Bangladesh v Australia. Action from the first T20 in the three-match series, which took place at Bir Shrestho Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Cricket Stadium in Chittagong.

programme.title: One Day International Cricket
programme.desc: India v Afghanistan. Action from the second ODI in the three-match series, which took place at Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow.

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: A look back at the one-off Test between India and Afghanistan.

programme.title: WRC Review
programme.desc: The best action from the 2026 World Rally Championship so far, including events in Monte Carlo, Sweden, Kenya, and Japan.

programme.title: UIM F1H2O World Championship Highlights
programme.desc: A look at the latest action in Italy.

programme.title: TNT Sports Reload
programme.desc: The best bits from TNT Sports over the last week, including exclusive interviews, top action, and more.

programme.title: Supercars Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the first day of the Tasmania Super 440.

programme.title: Supercars Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second day of the Tasmania Super 440.

programme.title: Live: V8 Supercars Championship
programme.desc: The Darwin Triple Crown. Coverage of race one at Hidden Valley Raceway, where the sixth round of the season takes place.

programme.title: Live: ABB FIA Formula E World Championship
programme.desc: The Sanya ePrix first practice session. Coverage of preparations for the 11th round of the season at Sanya Street Circuit in China.

programme.title: Live AFL
programme.desc: Gold Coast Suns v Hawthorn (Bounce-up 10.40am). Coverage of the match from the 15th round of fixtures at People First Stadium in Gold Coast.

programme.title: MLB Highlights
programme.desc: Action from Major League Baseball.

programme.title: MLB Plays of the Week
programme.desc: A look at the most exciting plays from the past week's matches.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Goals Reload
programme.desc: The pick of the goals from Arsenal's campaign.

programme.title: WRC Review
programme.desc: The best action from the 2026 World Rally Championship so far, including events in Monte Carlo, Sweden, Kenya, and Japan.

programme.title: It's A Numbers Game
programme.desc: The key numbers behind Valentino Rossi and Marc Marquez in MotoGP, Jonathan Rea's record?setting Superbike dominance, the rapid Thruxton circuit, and more.

programme.title: Live: Fencing European Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session on day four in Antony, France.

programme.title: Mass Participation World
programme.desc: Action from the Trans Sahara Marathon.

programme.title: Roland Garros
programme.desc: A look back at the 2026 tournament.

programme.title: Tom Daley: 1.6 Seconds
programme.desc: Tom Daley's journey from Olympic prodigy to national treasure, through athletic triumphs, losing his dad, coming out, and intense public pressure.

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the seventh round of the season at Misano World Circuit in Emilia-Romagna, Italy.

programme.title: FIA World Endurance Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 24 Hours of Le Mans, which served as the third round of the season at Circuit de la Sarthe in Le Mans, France.

programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: (If necessary).

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: The Pat McAfee Show
programme.desc: Former NFL punter Pat McAfee brings his humor and candor to the microphone to highlight the day's top stories.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: NBA Today
programme.desc: In-depth, comprehensive daily coverage of all aspects of the NBA from expert analysts.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.desc: From Great American Ball Park in Cincinnati.
